behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Agentic because correctness depends on real AI/ML transcription behavior (speaker diarization/label retention), not just deterministic wiring. MOCK-HIDE applies: a stubbed STT response with perfect labels would hide real diarization failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on one model-stage property, "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels," not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript segments retain stable speaker labels across turns; verify labels are present and mappable for downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Unit: this behavior is local parsing/transformation logic on transcript text to a structured object, with no required real OS/network/model dependency. Rejected Integration by ADJACENT-MIN-REPRO: a single parser function with fixed transcript input can reproduce failures.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixture transcripts containing explicit assignments and assert extracted records include exact assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Agentic because pass/fail depends on real LLM output quality (coverage of assigned attendees in generated action items). Deterministic mocks can always satisfy coverage and hide real omissions. Rejected Workflow: behavior text requires one output-quality check on the "live summary output" and does not require validating multiple agentic handoff stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with known per-attendee assignments to the live summarizer using the real LLM; assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Unit: this is deterministic routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified. Behavior text explicitly says "identified decisions," which disqualifies requiring real model judgment for this check. Rejected Integration: failure can be reproduced at one local function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a structured input containing identified decisions plus narrative text into the routing mapper and assert decisions are emitted only in `decisions` schema fields, not generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Agentic because correctness depends on real LLM extraction/placement decisions from transcript content. MOCK-HIDE applies: a canned model response would mask real misclassification of decisions into narrative text. Rejected Workflow: despite potential pipeline context, this behavior asks for one model-output property in the live summary, not a required multi-agentic-step sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM summarization on transcripts with clearly stated decisions; assert each key decision appears in the output `decisions` section and is not only present in narrative fields.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Workflow: behavior explicitly requires multiple sequential processing steps over segments plus merged coherence, i.e., chained agentic stages. TIE-BREAKER evidence for higher level: "splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary." Rejected Agentic because one model decision is insufficient; the failure can emerge from cross-segment sequencing/handoff/merge interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation, sequential STT+summary processing per segment, and merge; assert ordered segment processing occurred and final merged summary is structurally coherent and complete.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Workflow because this is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with multiple agentic steps (transcribe then summarize) plus delivery orchestration. Lower levels miss cross-stage failures and manual-intervention regressions. Rejected Agentic by behavior text: "full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output)" requires sequential multi-step validation, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real meeting recording from upload to delivered structured summary; assert successful completion, no manual steps, and valid final schema payload.
